contraventionDescription: Parked at an ‘out of order’ meter during controlled hours. If a meter shows out of order you are not permitted to park there – Contravention Code 08

sections.heading: How to have your penalty charge notice (PCN) for this parking contravention cancelled.
sections.content: We may be able to assist you in having your penalty charge notice for this contravention cancelled.
sections.contentHtml: We may be able to assist you in having your penalty charge notice for this contravention cancelled.

sections.heading: Follow our 3 stage process:
sections.content:
1 Check: Specific grounds for appeal for this contravention (below on this page)
This may also include diagrams and pictures showing signage requirements etc.
2 Check: General grounds for appeal
Applies to most contraventions.
3 Check: Summarised parking adjudicators decisions
Includes details of example cases won.
sections.contentHtml:
1 Check: Specific grounds for appeal for this contravention (below on this page)
This may also include diagrams and pictures showing signage requirements etc.

2 Check: General grounds for appeal
Applies to most contraventions.

3 Check: Summarised parking adjudicators decisions
Includes details of example cases won.

sections.heading: Specific grounds of appeal for this contravention
sections.content:
1. Were the words ‘out of order’ clearly shown at the time that you parked your car? It could have easily been the case that at the time that you had parked your car and inserted money, the machine did not display ‘out of order’. Meters can malfunction. If the meter was functioning correctly when you parked and paid then you should appeal. Sometimes a metre will suddenly display “out of order” after you have inserted your money. If this is the case you must move your car. You can claim back the lost money from the council.
2. You should note that this offence only applies during controlled hours. So you are entitled to park at an “out of order” meter outside of the controlled hours shown on the meter.
3. You can also cite compassionate grounds in any appeal.
Please see our section on parking adjudicators decisions which will have details of cases for this contravention.
Back to Parking
